mistakes: missing return on recursive call
wrong recursive relationship
qualitative.key_insight: base case is n===0, recursive case is n + sumToN(n-1)
qualitative.coach_notes: 3 attempts to mastery — pattern recognition wasn't there yet

qualitative.key_insight: pattern recognition kicking in from Sum 1 to N
qualitative.coach_notes: first attempt success — template internalized

qualitative.key_insight: reduce by n/2 instead of n-1 — same pattern, different progress mechanism
qualitative.coach_notes: first attempt success

mistakes: base case indexing error — n===1,n===2 instead of n===0,n===1
qualitative.key_insight: call tree visualization made overlapping subproblems obvious — fib(2) calculated twice, fib(1) three times
qualitative.coach_notes: visualization was highest-leverage moment — abstract concept clicked when made visual

mistakes: if(cache[n]) instead of if(n in cache) — falsy zero bug
qualitative.key_insight: store and reuse values instead of recomputing — self-articulated after seeing call tree
qualitative.coach_notes: self-caught the cache bug — the specific guided question unlocked it. 1.6M× speedup demonstrated (fib(50): 60s → 0.037ms)

mistakes: wrong function name in recursive call — caught himself when tests failed
empty string edge case caused infinite recursion — caught via tracing
qualitative.key_insight: both versions are O(n²) due to string concatenation — not because of slice. Raised this himself and concluded recursive string reversal is pointless in production
qualitative.coach_notes: strong self-advocacy — flagged that complexity analysis had been omitted and asked for it always

mistakes: triangle[i]=[1] on last element wipes the whole row — caught after one targeted trace question
said n(n-1)/2 instead of n(n+1)/2 for total cells formula
qualitative.key_insight: one is creating an element in the parent array, another is updating an element of the parent array
qualitative.coach_notes: tracing is the primary unblocking tool — one targeted question unlocked the overwrite bug himself

mistakes: inner loop started at row.length-1 instead of row.length-2 — caught via tracing i=1
qualitative.key_insight: right-to-left avoids overwriting values still needed. The newly pushed 1 should never be touched — start at row.length-2
qualitative.coach_notes: session was split (2am on a train) — still shipped it. Right-to-left in-place pattern recurs in knapsack, edit distance

mistakes: reverse loop condition left===right instead of left<right
break outside the if block — would always break after first outer loop iteration
mistook sequential loops for nested — called O(n²) instead of O(n)
qualitative.key_insight: never independently articulated one — full guided walkthrough. Understood each step when explained but could not derive algorithm cold
qualitative.coach_notes: explicit plan to redo cold after more two-pointer reps. Flag: sequential vs nested loop complexity

mistakes: if instead of while for skipping — only skips one non-alphanumeric
bounds check in wrong order — regex evaluated before the guard
qualitative.key_insight: left<right guard must come first — short-circuit evaluation means regex never runs when pointers have crossed
qualitative.coach_notes: 5th percentile runtime due to regex. Revisit with charCodeAt optimization. Recursive version deferred by Safwaan

mistakes: typo: num[i] instead of nums[i] — caught himself during trace
qualitative.key_insight: time-space trade-off: brute force O(n²)/O(1), hash map O(n)/O(n). Buying speed with memory
qualitative.coach_notes: came in planning to use two pointers — correctly identified himself that array is unsorted and two pointers don't apply

mistakes: declared left and right as const instead of let
multiple if checks instead of else if — caught the runtime difference himself (61st → 100th percentile)
qualitative.key_insight: sorted array: moving left always decreases sum, moving right always increases it. Without that guarantee you're just guessing — self-articulated
qualitative.coach_notes: needed nudge to reach for two pointers — wouldn't have identified pattern cold. But once given the sorted property insight, derived the rest independently

pattern.primary: Two pointers — both ends, fill backwards

mistakes: forgot to sort in naive solution (low sleep)
tried to place two elements per iteration — caught himself via tracing
left<right instead of left<=right — needed example, fixed immediately
right declared without let/const (implicit global) — not caught
qualitative.key_insight: largest squares always at the ends — never the middle. Compare both ends, place largest at back, one element per iteration
qualitative.coach_notes: low sleep day. Implicit global variable not caught — watch for this. Complexity reasoning improving — used pointer movement to reason O(n), not loop syntax

pattern.primary: Two pointers — converge, greedy move shorter

mistakes: width formula (j+1)-(i+1) instead of j-i — caught after trace of i=0,j=1
couldn't fully justify pointer movement without guided Socratic chain
qualitative.key_insight: moving the taller pointer is provably wasteful — height capped at shorter, width only shrinks. Only moving shorter gives any chance of improvement. Self-articulated after guided chain
qualitative.coach_notes: pointer movement justification still guided — probe this cold next time. left<right reasoning was solid and independent

mistakes: result.push[...] instead of result.push(...) — caught when asked to look at the push call
forgot to sort nums before two-pointer loop
break after first match — never finds additional triplets for same i
triplet declared outside while loop, never reset
skip conditions checked wrong neighbor — self-diagnosed correctly
single if instead of while for consecutive duplicates — flagged himself before it was raised
qualitative.key_insight: fix one element, two-pointer the rest for a pair summing to -nums[i]. Sort first — duplicates become adjacent so dedup is free. O(n²) time, O(1) extra space
qualitative.coach_notes: closes subproblems at first success — surfaced 3x. Independently decomposed to k-sum and called O(n³)/O(n²) unprompted. Toolkit gap: Set reference equality, Array.from not at fingertips

mistakes: typo num2Pointer
while condition <= instead of >=
read from nums1 instead of nums2 for nums2PointerValue
assigned pointer index instead of value
qualitative.key_insight: fill from the right — currentIndex always stays ahead of nums1Pointer so you never overwrite a value you still need
qualitative.coach_notes: all bugs self-caught via tracing. Slow start due to placeholder zeros confusion. Pattern transfer from LC 977 happened with one recall probe.

mistakes: missing p1++ for non-target case — infinite loop
double p1++ after fix — caught by tracing
termination condition swappableIndex===0 instead of <=p1 — required 40min trace to expose
return value n-valuesSwapped instead of p1 — self-caught from tracing [3,3,2]
qualitative.key_insight: loop must terminate when pointers cross (swappableIndex<=p1), not when one hits zero. Return p1 — it is the count of valid elements. Read/write: write pointer only moves on a write; safe to overwrite because write never gets ahead of read
qualitative.coach_notes: swap approach designed cold — harder of the two solutions. Tracing heuristic taught: use smallest input that exposes the bug. Read/write pattern new, derived in one attempt after setup.

mistakes: reached for swap/move-to-end approach initially — approach mismatch
used const for variable reassigned in inner while — flagged by question, not self-caught
unnecessary inner while loop — removed after one question
qualitative.key_insight: p2 reads every step; p1 only writes when nums[p2] !== nums[p1]. Sorted array means duplicates are adjacent, so one pass is enough.
qualitative.coach_notes: pattern-matched to LC 27 swap variant immediately — pivoted correctly once dead end was recognised. const reassignment habit recurring quietly.

problem.name: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array II

mistakes: group thinking — tracked occurrence counts and wrote on transition instead of per-element
spent significant time patching broken approach instead of scrapping it
qualitative.key_insight: think per-element not per-group: skip when both nums[p1-1] and nums[p1-2] equal the current value
qualitative.coach_notes: group thinking named as new mistake pattern. Bootstrap condition (p1<2) was self-observed. Return value (p1 not p1+1) self-reasoned.

mistakes: for...in keys are strings — needed Number(key), caught after one prompt
forgot for...in syntax cold (had to look up)
qualitative.key_insight: Boyer-Moore: keep one candidate and count; mismatch cancels a vote; majority survives because it appears more than half the time
qualitative.coach_notes: short clean session; pushed back on written trace after tests passed — accepted. Boyer-Moore couldn't be derived cold; correctly self-diagnosed.

mistakes: missed n=0 on first two iterative attempts
missed negative n on first attempt
called myPow(x, n/2) twice instead of storing — double work
scoped powerOfHalfed inside if/else — inaccessible outside
n/2 without Math.floor for odd n — float causes infinite recursion
qualitative.key_insight: x^n = x^(n/2) * x^(n/2) — halving the exponent each step gives O(log n); store the half once, square it; odd n adds one extra x
qualitative.coach_notes: pattern was brand new — he exhausted his toolbox honestly and asked for help. Good metacognition. Math intuition solid; knowledge gap, not reasoning gap.

mistakes: if(seen[currentElement]) fails for 0 — falsy check on plain object, caught when prompted
qualitative.key_insight: Set for membership tracking — no falsy edge case, no string conversion overhead. HashMap when you also need to store a value.
qualitative.coach_notes: Reached for HashMap directly without brute force — pattern recognition shift. Independently identified Set as cleaner choice. Self-articulated Set vs HashMap distinction.

mistakes: wrong index in right scan (height[rightMaxHeight] instead of height[rightStartIndex])
infinite loop in left scan — leftStartIndex not decrementing in else branch
two-pointer condition (leftMax <= rightMax) not derived — got from external chat
qualitative.key_insight: min(maxLeft, maxRight) - height[i]; precompute both maxes in two passes to eliminate inner scans; two-pointer processes the bottleneck side without needing both maxes upfront
qualitative.coach_notes: prefix max derivation was entirely independent and clean — highlight of the session. Two-pointer condition gap acknowledged honestly. Hard redo scheduled 2026-06-13.

problem.name: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock

mistakes: last-day edge case on brute force — maxSellingPrice stays -1 when inner loop never runs
tried to fix decreasing prices by corrupting maxSellingPrice to 0 inside inner loop
if/else instead of Math.max() — self-identified habit
qualitative.key_insight: scan left to right, track running minimum, profit at each step = current - minPrice, keep the best
qualitative.coach_notes: needed full trace walkthrough before code structure clicked; abstract understanding was there but could not start the loop. Self-identified Math.max() avoidance habit.

mistakes: copy-paste variable name in t branch (currSChar instead of currTChar)
else-branch initial value for t was 1 instead of -1
comparison loop iterated s only, missing t-only characters with negative counts
qualitative.key_insight: one-map comparison must iterate Object.keys() not just s — t-only characters accumulate negative values that never get checked otherwise
qualitative.coach_notes: clean session; increment/decrement insight clicked fast when suggested; comparison loop bug needed a counter-example to land

pattern.primary: Hash Map — sorted string as key

mistakes: swapped if/else branches — creating array in the exists branch, pushing in the new branch
qualitative.key_insight: sort each string's characters to get a canonical key — all anagrams produce the same sorted form; use as HashMap key, push originals into the array at that key
qualitative.coach_notes: sorted-key insight needed to be given; first instinct was O(n²) pairwise using isAnagram as helper. Once insight landed, execution was clean.

mistakes: reset subString to empty instead of slicing from dupIndex+1
maxLength not updated in else branch
currentWindowSize computed before left updated
left = map[char]+1 without Math.max — pointer jumping backwards
const on reassigned variables (recurring)
pulled Set solution from online mid-session
qualitative.key_insight: sliding window: expand right always, shrink left when duplicate found; HashMap stores char→last index for O(1) jump; Math.max(left, map[char]+1) ensures left never goes backwards
qualitative.coach_notes: window concept needed visual trace before code clicked; Math.max guard was given not derived — probe cold at LC 424

qualitative.key_insight: count what ransomNote needs, spend magazine characters against it — positive count means magazine came up short
qualitative.coach_notes: fastest clean solve of the sprint. Explored 5 variants. Added splice, charCodeAt, ?? vs || to toolkit.

mistakes: one-map namespace collision — s-side key shadowed t-side has-check
map.set before map.has — wrong order in else branch
tMapping comparison used currentTChar instead of currentSChar
stale variable name map.set after renaming to sMapping/tMapping
qualitative.key_insight: s-side keys and t-side keys live in separate namespaces — one map collapses them; two maps keeps them clean; check for conflict then set unconditionally
qualitative.coach_notes: namespace collision concept took a full trace of paper/title to make concrete; clean two-line pattern shown at end and immediately understood

mistakes: .set() argument order — used wrong variable as key, omitted value in both maps
missing n !== s.length length check — self-caught before submitting
qualitative.key_insight: two-map bijection transfers directly from LC 205; only difference is s.split(" ") to get words array
qualitative.coach_notes: bijection pattern landing; why-one-map-fails answered cold and perfectly before attempting

mistakes: window.size > k+1 too loose — allows k+2 elements before shrinking
deleted currentElement instead of nums[l] when shrinking
initial confusion: tried to store all previous indices per value instead of just most recent
qualitative.key_insight: maintain a Set window of size k; shrink when r-l>k by deleting nums[l]; for HashMap store value→last index and always update it
qualitative.coach_notes: went back to notes/video mid-session when HashMap wasn't clicking — good metacognition. Did verbose-to-terse HashMap compression independently across 3 versions.

mistakes: = instead of += in accumulator — self-caught after one question
new Set(n) instead of new Set([n]) — Set takes an iterable
used .push() and .length on a Set — Set has .add() and .size
starting value -1 instead of n
forgot String() conversion after removing array
passed original n instead of currentValue to sumOfSquares helper
did not independently connect loop-forever to repeated-value — needed one nudge
qualitative.key_insight: cycle detection = track seen states in a Set; digit extraction via % 10 and Math.floor avoids string allocation
qualitative.coach_notes: one nudge needed for cycle detection insight; immediately reached for Set and avoided O(n) includes on his own once prompted

mistakes: const on mutable accumulators
for...in on a Set instead of for...of
elem.get() and seen.get() on a Set
forgot to advance curr (would have caused infinite loop)
forgot to reset runningSequenceCount
forgot return statement
qualitative.key_insight: only count from elements where n-1 is not in Set; inner while amortizes to O(n) total across all sequences
qualitative.coach_notes: for...in vs for...of explicitly flagged as a gap — needs deliberate drilling; Set API confusion (.get() on Set) recurred from 3Sum

qualitative.key_insight: a^a=0 and a^0=a, combined with XOR's commutativity/associativity, means XOR-ing the whole array cancels every duplicate pair and leaves only the single survivor
qualitative.coach_notes: first zero-mistake, zero-hint session of the sprint; directly attributable to having pre-built a bit manipulation cheat sheet before attempting the problem; bonus problem pulled forward from Phase 18 given interview proximity

mistakes: assumed bitwise operators require explicit decimal-to-binary conversion
missing reassignment on right shift (n >>> 1; instead of n = n >>> 1;)
qualitative.key_insight: integers are already stored in binary internally — bitwise operators act on that representation directly, no conversion step needed; n & 1 reads the last bit, n = n >>> 1 advances to the next
qualitative.coach_notes: second bit-manipulation problem same day as LC 136; needed direct correction on the binary-conversion misconception (knowledge gap, not reasoning gap); self-caught the missing-assignment bug immediately when prompted; explicitly self-identified log-based complexity reasoning as a weak spot and needed a full walkthrough from O(log n) to the O(1) fixed-width framing

mistakes: confused rotation with reversal
proposed OR-then-shift instead of shift-then-OR
proposed OR-then-right-shift (discards bits)
while(n!==0) terminated before all 32 positions processed
used n >> 1 instead of n >>> 1
qualitative.key_insight: reversing mirrors bit positions (i -> n-1-i), not rotates; building a positioned result requires shift-then-OR (never the reverse) and a fixed 32-iteration loop rather than a while(n!==0) loop
qualitative.coach_notes: first bit-manipulation bonus needing real debugging; trace tables (not verbal explanation) were the explicit unlock for the shift/OR ordering confusion — Safwaan confirmed this directly and asked for more of it; also independently read the actual problem constraints (n is even) and correctly reasoned why >>> 0 wasn't needed for this variant; strong instinct shown pushing back on a fix before accepting it

mistakes: conditional add instead of unconditional expand
recording after while loop (invalid window)
while condition > instead of >=
missing Infinity to 0 return
qualitative.key_insight: always add nums[end] unconditionally, then shrink while sum >= target — the add is never conditional
qualitative.coach_notes: needed explicit scaffold to get expand-always structure; zero self-caught bugs; first session back after ~1 week break

problem.name: Substring with Concatenation of All Words

pattern.primary: Sliding Window — Fixed Size, word frequency

mistakes: character frequency instead of word frequency (anagram instinct misapplied)
words[i] undefined reference in window size
words[1] instead of words[0].length
copied words into windowFreq instead of building from s
j += 2 instead of j++
wrong substring args (missing i offset, end vs length)
wordFreq === windowFreq reference comparison
s.subString wrong capitalization
off-by-one: < s.length - k missed last valid position
qualitative.key_insight: atomic unit is words not characters; all words same length so slice s into wordLen chunks at i + j*wordLen; compare word-frequency maps entry by entry
qualitative.coach_notes: anagram instinct is sticky — watch for correct vs incorrect application at LC 567 (which IS character-based); Map comparison was a complete gap, gave isMapsEqual directly; fixed vs variable window distinction confused him

pattern.primary: DFS — Accumulate Over Tree (iterative pairs + recursive post-order)

qualitative.key_insight: direct pattern transfer from max-root-to-leaf-path-sum: push [node, depth] pairs so branches carry independent state; recursive: Math.max(left, right) + 1, null → 0
qualitative.coach_notes: cleanest solve to date — zero hints, zero false starts, both approaches written simultaneously. Introduced balanced vs skewed tree terminology during complexity discussion.

mistakes: flattening to arrays loses structure
short-circuit return true before checking subtrees
missing null base case
qualitative.key_insight: matching values at the current node is not enough — must verify both subtrees too. Null+null = both trees ended at the same point = agree
qualitative.coach_notes: base case reasoning unlocked by concrete call-stack trace, not verbal explanation. Short-circuit bug is same shape as closing-subproblem-at-first-success pattern.

qualitative.key_insight: swap current node children first (pre-order), then recurse — post-order and BFS also work, same result
qualitative.coach_notes: self-identified recursive returns as a gap; independently questioned traversal order and whether other approaches work — good metacognitive habit forming

qualitative.key_insight: mirror = cross-compare children: left.left↔right.right (outside) and left.right↔right.left (inside). One line different from Same Tree.
qualitative.coach_notes: needed concept explained visually; independently asked for alternative approaches and algorithm names — good metacognitive habit forming
